scope: Add IntegratedDiskPreemptFaultRecord as first-class integrated scenario evidence
Tie completed block pending IO EIO policy to timer-attributed preemption evidence
Require exact generations for preemption, timer interrupt, policy, block wait, wait token, request, device, and range evidence
Reject generic cancel policy and pending wait leaks for the same block request generation
Emit IntegratedDiskPreemptFaultRecorded event and stable osctl ViewV1 JSON
Expose historical graph edges without creating live ownership or adapter-internal semantic truth
Publish target_executor semantic package roots and count validation for X2 evidence
no_goals: real virtio-blk, DMA, MMIO, or IRQ execution
new third-party block or filesystem dependency

implemented_contract_clauses.clause: X2 disk pending IO fault under preemption
all integrated disk/preempt references carry kind/id/generation
integrated scenario uses Historical graph edges for evidence attribution
pending block IO failure is visible as cancelled block wait and wait token evidence
osctl exposes stable ViewV1 JSON and contract validation output

findings_fixed: osctl command and inspect dispatch expose integrated-disk-preempt-fault JSON
contract graph resolves X2 evidence through exact preemption, timer, block pending IO policy, block wait, wait token, request, device, and range generations
target_executor writes X2 roots/counts into the semantic package so root validation covers the new evidence
history graph emits Historical edges only for integrated disk/preempt attribution
semantic validation rejects generic cancel policy and pending block wait leaks for the same request generation
bad_smells_checked: No real disk fault execution is claimed by the integrated record
No virtio-blk or block adapter internal state became semantic truth
No substrate mapping is treated as semantic authority
No third-party dependency was introduced in semantic_core
osctl emits stable ViewV1 JSON rather than internal Rust structs
residual_notes: X3 owns SIMD task migration across harts
Future real disk/preemption runtime must replace semantic evidence sources without changing the X2 object identity contract
driver_store is exposed as semantic wait owner metadata, not a target Store live edge

validation_commands.command: cargo check -p semantic_core -p artifact_manifest -p contract_core -p target_executor -p osctl

residual_gaps: X3 owns SIMD task migration across harts
Real preemptive disk runtime must later provide substrate-backed timer and block fault evidence
